content.full_text:
May 2, 1977

Richard Bryant
Los Angeles County Election Department
808 North Spring Street
Los Angeles, CA 90012

Dear Mr. Bryant:

This letter will confirm my advice to you by telephone on April 25, 1977, concerning Government Code Section 81005(b).

Committees which support or oppose more than one candidate for Superior Court Judge, member of the State Legislature or Board of Equalization, must file campaign disclosure statements with the following officials:

Original and one copy - Secretary of State

Two copies - Clerk of the one county with the largest population (in whole or in part) in all of the election districts in which the candidates the filer supports or opposes seek nomination or election

Two copies - Clerk of the county of the filing committee's domicile

A committee which files copies of its first pre-election statement with your office is not also required to file copies of its second pre-election and post-election statements with you if Los Angeles County is not the largest county by population in the election districts of the candidates supported or opposed during the periods covered by such reports. Consequently, you are unable to determine for certain whether a committee has negligently or inadvertently failed to file a campaign statement or whether the committee did not support or oppose a candidate in Los Angeles County. The Commission is aware of the problem this filing scheme presents for election officials.

Presently, the staff can only suggest that election officials ask committees to advise them that they will not be filing with them again unless their county is largest by population in the election districts of the candidates supported or opposed. Since committees are not legally required to furnish such information, it would be done on a voluntary basis only.

I hope this information clarifies your question. If we can be of further assistance, please contact our office.

Sincerely yours,

Nan Emmerson

Nan Emmerson
Special Compliance Representative

NE:jg
